D4 Colorimetry L5 Evaluate Reference

Lightness-Preserving Clip

compare a naive per-channel RGB clamp against an LCh clip that keeps lightness and hue fixed while only chroma gives way

Clamping each RGB channel to [0,1] is the lazy fix — but it drags lightness and hue off target. The colour-managed answer works in LCh: hold L* and hue, and pull chroma inward only as far as the gamut boundary. Same hue, same lightness, just less vivid.

Set an out-of-gamut colour. The L*–C plane at this hue shows the gamut boundary; the LCh clip drops straight down in chroma (lightness held), while the RGB clamp drifts off in both lightness and hue. Compare the two patches.
